entries.title: On Acts 5:19
entries.excerpt: Acts IX. 10, 12 And there was a certain disciple at Damascus, named Ananias; and to him said the Lord in a vision, Ananias. And he said, Behold, I am here, Lord. And the Lord said to him, Arise, and go into the street which is called Straight, and inquire in the house of Judas for one called Saul, of Tarsus: for, behold, he prays, and has seen in a vision a man named Ananias coming in, and putting his hand on him, that he might receive his sight. What may be the reason that He neither drew any one of high authority and importance, nor caused such to be forthcoming for the purpose of…
